creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
After I faced the very same problem on another instance of www/owncloud which we updated - and rendered it useless - I tried to investigate more.

The observation is, that the update went in all cases to wreck the installation of onwcloud. In the second case, the connection to OpenLDAP (another instance on another server system) has not been been crippled, but afterwards login and use of the owncloud system wasn't possible anymore - refelcted by a message shown up on the entry screen of the browser, when contacting the owncloud service!

In either case, checking by the administrative OCC comannd via the command sequence

su -m www -c 'php ./occ COMMAND'

in which command is upgrade or app:list for instance, I do not get the LDAP module with its command options listed in case COMMAD is empty forcing occ to print out all possibilities, althoug when performing 

su -m www -c 'php ./occ app:list'

user_ldap: 0.9.0

is within the list of modules enabled.


Investigating the logging file I find this disturbing:

[...]
{"reqId":"DHf4SyCQW6opb8VGgUOq","remoteAddr":"","app":"PHP","message":"ldap_start_tls(): Unable to start TLS: Can't contact LDAP server at \/usr\/local\/www\/owncloud\/apps\/user_ldap\/lib\/LDAP.php#255","level":3,"time":"2016-11-16T10:08:14+00:00","method":"--","url":"--","user":"--"}

The target OpenLDAP server (at the moment the most recent, newly recompiled installation of net/openldap24-server, working with all other applications and servers, including Apache 2.4, Login, SAMBA 4.44!) is up and working. It seems, the OwnCloud LDAP module has a serious problem here I can not fathom.
